title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen and Professor Adler discuss the background of Krulmit, also known as Crocetta, noting his history of managing small businesses in America. Four years prior in Chattanooga, Tennessee, Crocetta partnered with a man named Harkness, or Stray, to run a "sun cult" that sold "sun pills" made of cod liver oil. Following the murder of Andrew Van, Crocetta and Van's servant, Klint, disappeared without a trace. Ellery notes that while Crocetta investigated a potential alliance between himself and Klint, he found no evidence of such a connection.

The conversation shifts to Alva Strach, a famous Egyptologist believed to be a harmless madman after suffering heatstroke in the Valley of the Kings. Although the Hanover County District Attorney considers Strach innocent of murdering Andrew Van, Ellery believes the evidence points toward Wilga Crosse as the killer. Ellery argues that the key to the case is identifying the true identity of Wilga Crosetti, suggesting the person could be anyone, including Krosok, who had previously positioned the Cult of the Sun near Bradwood. Ellery also theorizes that the motive might involve superstitions regarding decapitation and vampires, which are common in the Balkans and Central Europe.

Finally, the two debate the meaning of a T-shaped symbol. Ellery suggests the *crux commissa* is an "Egyptian cross" based on his research in the *Encyclopaedia Britannica*. Professor Adler corrects him, explaining that while T-shaped symbols appear in Egyptian hieroglyphics, the term "Egyptian cross" is a misnomer; the true cross-shaped symbol associated with ancient Egypt is the ankh.
